The soul thrives when we have a clear understanding of truth as it is in Jesus. This happens when we not only believe with our hearts but are also ready, when called, to make a public declaration with our mouths. Knowledge and faith enrich the soul. The stronger our faith and the warmer our love, the greater our comfort will be. The treasures of wisdom are hidden, not from us, but for us, in Christ. These were hidden from proud unbelievers but revealed in the person and redemption of Christ. Be careful of enticing words; many people are ruined by the false disguises and appealing appearances of evil principles and wicked practices! Be aware and cautious of those who would entice you to do wrong; they aim to deceive you. All Christians, at least in their profession, have received Jesus Christ as Lord, agreed with him, and accepted him as theirs. We cannot be strengthened in Christ or grow in him unless we are first rooted in him or founded upon him. Once established in the faith, we must increase in it and improve more and more. God justly withdraws this benefit from those who do not receive it with gratitude; and giving thanks for his kindnesses is what God rightfully requires.

There is a way of thinking that rightly uses our reasoning abilities; a study of God’s works, which leads us to the knowledge of God and confirms our faith in him. But there is also a philosophy that is empty and deceptive; it may please people's imaginations, but it hinders their faith. This includes curious speculations about things beyond us or not important to us. Those who follow the ways of the world turn away from following Christ. We have in Him the substance of all the shadows of the ceremonial law. All the shortcomings of that law are made up in the gospel of Christ through his complete sacrifice for sin and by revealing God’s will. To be complete means to be provided with everything necessary for salvation. This one word “complete” shows that we have in Christ whatever is required. “In him,” not as if he were distant from us, but we are united to him when, by the power of the Spirit, faith is worked in our hearts by the Spirit and we are joined to our Head. The circumcision of the heart, the crucifixion of the flesh, death and burial to sin and to the world, and resurrection to new life, which are shown through baptism and by faith working in our hearts, prove that our sins are forgiven and that we are fully delivered from the curse of the law. Through Christ, those who were dead in sins are made alive. Christ’s death was the end of our sins; Christ's resurrection is the quickening of our souls. The law of ordinances, which was a burden to the Jews and a barrier between them and the Gentiles, Jesus took away. When the substance arrived, the shadows disappeared. Since every mortal man is guilty of death through the requirements of the law, how terrible is the condition of ungodly people who trample underfoot the blood of God’s Son, by which alone this deadly handwriting can be erased! Do not be troubled by narrow-minded judgments about food or Jewish customs. Setting aside a portion of our time for worship and service to God is a moral and unchanging duty but does not depend on keeping the seventh day of the week, the Sabbath of the Jews. The first day of the week, or the Lord’s Day, is the day Christians keep holy in remembrance of Christ's resurrection. All Jewish rituals were shadows of gospel blessings.

It may seem humble to appeal to angels, as if people are aware of their unworthiness to speak directly to God. But this is not acceptable; it is giving honor that belongs only to Christ and assigning it to a creation. There is real pride in this seeming humility. Those who worship angels deny Christ, who is the only Mediator between God and man. It is an insult to Christ, who is the Head of the church, to use any intercessors but him. When people let go their hold on Christ, they grasp at things that will not help them. The body of Christ is a growing body. And true believers cannot conform to the ways of the world. True wisdom is to stick closely to the teachings of the gospel; in complete submission to Christ, who is the only Head of his church. Self-imposed sufferings and fastings might appear to show unusual spirituality and willingness for suffering, but this was not “to God’s honor.” It tended, in a wrong way, to satisfy the carnal mind by gratifying self-will, self-wisdom, self-righteousness, and contempt for others. These things carry so little of the appearance of wisdom that they do no good to the soul and do not provide for satisfying the flesh. What the Lord has left indifferent, let us regard as such and leave others to the same freedom; and remembering how fleeting earthly things are, let us seek to glorify God in their use.
